# Shell Over Edge

Temporary shell access over HTTPS.

## Flow

- Ask the user to start an agent on the target.
- POSIX: `curl -sS https://soe.stoff.dev | sh`
- PowerShell: `irm https://soe.stoff.dev/a.ps1 | iex`
- Use the printed 8-character code.
- Send raw: `curl -sS -X POST https://soe.stoff.dev/api/sessions/<code>/send --data 'pwd'`
- Send with cwd/timeout: `curl -sS -X POST 'https://soe.stoff.dev/api/sessions/<code>/send?timeout=30' --data '{"body":"pwd","cwd":"/tmp"}'`
- Read successful `/send` responses as plain text.
- End: `curl -sS -X POST https://soe.stoff.dev/api/sessions/<code>/end`

## API

- `GET /`: POSIX agent script.
- `GET /a.ps1`: PowerShell agent script.
- `POST /api/sessions/<code>/send?timeout=30`: send one command; body is raw text or `{"body":"pwd","cwd":"/tmp"}`.
- `POST /api/sessions/<code>/end`: close the session.

## Rules

- No bearer tokens, URL tokens, or invented codes.
- Timeout belongs in `?timeout=30`; JSON only supports `body` and `cwd`.
- Do not call retired endpoints: `/commands`, `/events`, `/upload`, `/download`, `/api/agent/*`, `/start/*`, `/connect.sh`.
- Keep commands explicit and reversible. Close sessions with `/end`.

## Limits

TTL 2 hours. Command body 64 KB. Result body 1 MB. Timeout 1-50 seconds.
